item.title: Secrets management (.env store)
item.intent: Ensure every credential written to disk is atomic, 0600-permissioned, and survives process interruption. No partial writes, no world-readable secrets.
item.description: SECURITY BASELINE: If .env is misconfigured or readable by other processes, the user's API keys are exposed. Atomic writes (temp + rename) and 0600 permissions are non-negotiable. This is the lowest layer of the credentials stack — everything CQ-008 (health probes) does depends on reliable .env reads. Acceptance: written files are always 0600; atomic write survives process kill mid-write; get/set/remove preserve format and comments.
steps: Implement .env file parser/reader/writer with format preservation
Add atomic writes via temp file + rename with 0600 permissions
acceptanceCriteria: Atomic write survives process kill mid-write (no partial files)
Get/set/remove preserve existing format and comments
